summary: Added a Quest Cockpit to each lane Overview so the selected money path opens with world, level, checkpoint, current gate, next move, recent events, and fast jumps into deeper lane views.

queries: quest log dashboard timeline UX game progression task management design patterns
interactive timeline dashboard drill down user experience best practices
game progression map UI task management dashboard unlock levels
pattern_notes: Quest logs should work as evolving roadmaps that combine objectives, context, progress markers, and reward feedback.
Interactive dashboards should support drill-down so the first view stays readable while deeper evidence remains reachable.
Progression systems scale cleanly when they expose hierarchy such as world, level, and checkpoint.
Timeline patterns are useful for activity history when they preserve sequence, status, and source context.
Complex dashboards benefit from story-first hierarchy and progressive disclosure.

ui_contract: Render Quest Cockpit at the top of the lane Overview before the World Module.
Show world, level, and checkpoint hierarchy as quest stages.
Show asks, unlocks, events, and score as compact lane signals.
Show one Next Move card and one Current Gate card derived from the same lane data used by Chronicle and Comms.
Show up to four recent lane events as an event chain.
PATH, LOG, GAME, COM, and TRL controls use existing data-detail-view routing and remain local-only.
Use the lane texture, accent colors, avatar, and existing card geometry so new lanes inherit the cockpit automatically.

tdd.red: Playwright behavior check first failed with cockpit=false, stages=0, controls=0, gate=false, next=false.
tdd.green: After implementation the same behavior check passed with cockpit=true, stages=3, controls=5, gate=true, next=true, events=4, and no horizontal overflow.

checks: 390x920 viewport: Quest Cockpit rendered 3 stages, 4 signals, 4 events, 5 controls, two-column stage/event grids, no clipped cockpit text, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.

1280x800 viewport: Quest Cockpit rendered the full desktop layout with 3 stages, 4 signals, 4 events, 5 controls, no clipped cockpit text, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.

1600x900 viewport: Quest Cockpit rendered inside the narrow side detail panel with two-column stage/event grids, no clipped cockpit text, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
PATH control changed the route to #lane=platform_engineering&view=path and rendered Path Map.
COM control changed the route to #lane=platform_engineering&view=comms and rendered the command deck.
